text:
Eight years after her first Olympic all-around gold medal in Rio,Simone Bilesof the U.S. was crowned once again the all-around champion at the Bercy arena in Paris on Thursday evening.

With her explosive display of difficulty, Biles, 27, delivered under pressure to record 59.131 for her 9thOlympic medal of her career (sixth gold). Biles had an uncharacteristic mistake on bars after her Pak salto, and scored only 13.733, but rebounded with the highest scores of the competition on beam 14.5 and floor 15.066 to take the title. “Oh gosh, I mean, I’ve put in so many numbers on the bars. So it was an unfortunate mistake, but I knew to just keep pushing and to not give up. And that’s exactly what you saw out there”, Biles said.

Biles also received the top score on vault 15.766 for her Yurchenko double pike. “I’m just ecstatic with my performances tonight. And we still have three finals left for me. Now it’s time to have fun and the hard part is over “, she said.

Biles has qualified to the vault, balance beam and the floor finals. She won the vault and floor Olympic gold medals in Rio, and is two time Olympic bronze medalist on balance beam.

No gymnast had ever repeated as the all-around champion in non-consecutive Olympic games, which makes Biles the first one to do so.

With the win Biles also becomes only the third female to have won the Olympic all-around twice, joining Soviet Union’sLarisa LatyninaandVěra Čázlavskáof Czechoslovakia.

Biles also continues a dominant streak for her country, as for the last 20 years now at the Olympics the all-around title has been won by a U.S. gymnast.Carly Pattersonwon in 2004, followed byNastia Liukinin 2008,Gabby Douglasin 2012, Biles in 2016 andSuni Leein 2020.



Brazil’sRebeca Andradeearned the silver medal and repeated as the all-around silver medalist, while defending champion Suni Lee of the U.S. earned the bronze.

“I’m just very happy and grateful. It was a tough competition. It’s very difficult to have three days of competition (qualification, team final and then the individual all-around), but I’m very happy with how I performed”, the silver medalist said. “I knew that if I focused on myself, I would get the result that I wanted. Everything went like planned. That’s why I am so happy about the result”, she added.

Andrade’s score was more than respectable 57.932, while Lee posted 56.465 to narrowly edgeAlice D’Amatoof Italy who finished fourth with 56.333.

Kaylia Nemourof Algeria was fifth with 55.899, the highest ever finish for an Algerian gymnast at the Olympics. Nemour recorded the top score of the final on uneven bars with 15.533.

Ellie Blackof Canada was sixth with 54.799, followed byQiu Qiyuanof China with 54.766 andHelen Kevricof Germany with 54.598.

Lee and Biles also made history at the start of the final, as no two female Olympic all-around champions have ever competed in the same all-around final at the Olympics.

The gymnastics competitions at the Olympic Games in Paris continue on August 3, with the beginning of the individual event finals.

2024 Paris Olympic GamesWomen’s all-around results:

Vanesa Mašová of Czechia teamed with U.S. gymnast David Shamah to capture the mixed-pairs Sokol Grand Prix, one of two competitions held in memory of legendary Czech gymnast Vera Caslavska this weekend in Brno, Czechia.

After notching perfect scores in two of their three performances, Olympic gold medalist Jordan Chiles and professional dance partner Ezra Sosa earned third place in ABC’s “Dancing with the Stars” season finale on Tuesday.

Double victor Camilo Vera of Colombia, Xiang Yina of China, Nishiyama Misa of Japan, Ojima Nao of Japan and AIN Arsenii Dukhno captured titles on the second of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Monday.

China and the U.S. each claimed two golds, and an AIN gymnast took the fifth title, on the first of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Sunday.
